note: ColumnID is the stable write key. The renamed-columns trap: rc_update_row / setRowProperties accepts custom columns keyed by ColumnID ONLY (e.g. {"1":"..."}), never by display name ({"graphics":"..."} is silently dropped). rc_get_rows READS these columns back under lowercase aliases (graphics, format, lastline, notes) but the WRITE endpoint does not accept those names. Critical write columns: 1=Graphics, 2=Format, 4=Last Line, 9=Notes. Columns 5,6,7,8,10 exist in the data model but are unused/undisplayed in the current layout (LayoutID 1) and should not be written to.
discrepancy_flag: The rundown-creator skill documents the API-DEFAULT column names (1=Writer, 2=Editor, 4=Talent, 9=VTR). The LIVE rc_list_columns 'Name' field already returns the DRM display names below (Graphics/Format/Last Line/Notes). The ColumnIDs are the invariant either way; the skill's default-name table is the pre-rename API baseline, not what this account returns today.

columns.name: Format
columns.role: Graphic type from Tracker, verbatim (asset type)

columns.name: Last Line
columns.role: Spoken phrase that cues the NEXT row's graphic
